laws.actName: The Governors (Emoluments, Allowances and Privileges) Amendment Act, 2014 (No. 8 of 2014)
laws.title: Amendment of section 2
laws.description: Substitutes clause (a) with new definitions for 'ex-Governor' and 'Governor'. 'ex-Governor' refers to a person who has served as Governor for one or more States. 'Governor' includes those discharging the functions of the Governor for one or more States.
laws.simpleExplanation: This section updates the legal definitions of who counts as a current Governor or a former (ex) Governor under the law[cite: 9].

relatedSections.description: The definition of 'ex-Governor' determines eligibility for the secretarial assistance provided in the new section 12A[cite: 9].

laws.actName: The Governors (Emoluments, Allowances and Privileges) Amendment Act, 2014 (No. 8 of 2014)
laws.title: Entitlement of ex-Governor to secretarial assistance
laws.description: Inserts section 12A, granting an ex-Governor entitlement to secretarial assistance of one Personal Assistant on a reimbursement basis for the remainder of their life. This entitlement is suspended if the ex-Governor is re-appointed to the office of Governor, elected to Parliament/State Legislature, or appointed to an office of profit.
laws.simpleExplanation: Former Governors get a personal assistant paid for by the government for life. However, if they get a new government job or are elected to office, they lose this perk while they are working that new job[cite: 9].

relatedSections.description: Amended to allow the government to make specific rules regarding how this reimbursement and assistance are handled[cite: 9].

laws.actName: The Governors (Emoluments, Allowances and Privileges) Amendment Act, 2014 (No. 8 of 2014)
laws.title: Amendment of section 13
laws.description: Amends sub-section (2) of section 13 to include clause (h), which empowers the creation of rules regarding the manner of providing secretarial assistance and reimbursement under section 12A.
laws.simpleExplanation: This gives the government the power to create official rules on how the personal assistant benefits for former Governors will actually be managed and paid back[cite: 9].
